summary: An excellent matrix mix delivers a clean, mellow sound that showcases standout performances of Peggy‑O, Candyman and The Other One.
review: The show is praised as a solid, fun performance with Jerry’s vocals and the band’s tight playing highlighted throughout; Peggy‑O, Candyman, Bertha > Good Lovin’ and The Other One receive special acclaim. Hunter’s matrix mix is described as crisp, natural and free of reverb while still preserving crowd ambience, making it a pleasure to listen to on headphones.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are strongly positive about both the musical performance and the matrix recording quality; the weighted average of show (≈5) and recording (≈5) yields a near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with no added reverb; natural balance between board clarity and subtle crowd ambience; soft, tasteful drums on slower numbers; overall very listen‑friendly.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rousing start with a spectacular Bertha > Good Lovin' segue, mellow mid‑set vibes, and a fun first Werewolves of London; the band maintains high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals praised as strong alongside Donna; guitar work implied by overall instrumental tightness.
band_member_comments.Drums: Soft, tasteful drumming noted especially on Peggy‑O; overall drum sound described as mellow.
